senses.definition: a kitchen tool used to remove the core and seeds from apples, leaving the flesh intact for various culinary purposes
a kitchen tool designed to cut apples into slices or wedges for easy and convenient snacking or cooking
a special device or knife for removing the skin of vegetables or fruit
a kitchen tool used to suction up juices or liquid from a container and then dispense them over food for basting
a small tool used to open the metal top of a bottle

examples.x: He grabbed a bottle opener to open his drink.
The kitchen drawer had a metal bottle opener.
She bought a keychain with a built-in bottle opener.
The bartender used a bottle opener to serve the drinks quickly.
They forgot to bring a bottle opener to the picnic.

senses.definition: a long, serrated knife designed specifically for slicing through bread and other baked goods without crushing or squishing them
a device used to slice cheese into thin, uniform pieces, typically consisting of a cutting wire or blade and a handle
a lightweight, gauzy fabric commonly used in cooking and food preparation to strain liquids, bundle herbs and spices, and wrap cheese
a kitchen tool specifically designed for removing pits from cherries, making it easier to enjoy the fruit without the pits
a plastic or metal bowl with many holes that is used for separating water from washed or cooked food
a small tool with a pointy spiral metal for pulling out corks from bottles

examples.x: She struggled for a moment before remembering to use the corkscrew to open the bottle of wine for dinner.
The picnic basket was fully equipped with plates, napkins, and a corkscrew for the bottle of red they had brought.
He handed the corkscrew to his friend, who expertly twisted it into the cork and pulled it out with a satisfying pop.
After searching through the kitchen drawers, he finally found the corkscrew tucked away in the back.
The bartender reached for a corkscrew to open the new bottle of Chardonnay, skillfully extracting the cork without breaking it.

senses.definition: a wooden or plastic board on which meat or vegetables are cut
a glass object showing three to five minutes by sand flow, used to measure the time it takes to boil eggs
a kitchen tool with a broad and flat part on one end, used for turning and lifting food
a flat kitchen utensil with a long handle used to lift and flip foods, such as pancakes, burgers, and vegetables, while cooking
a cone-shaped kitchen tool used for pouring liquids or powders into a container with a small opening without spilling
a small handheld kitchen tool used for crushing garlic
a kitchen tool having a surface with sharped holes used for cutting food into very small pieces
a kitchen tool with many holes used for separating solid materials from liquids
a handheld tool with a conical ridged end used for manually extracting juice from citrus fruits like lemons, limes, and oranges
a handheld tool designed to extract juice from fruits and vegetables by applying pressure to the fruit while it is placed in the device
a container with numbers on it for measuring the quantity of something when cooking, used mainly in the US
a kitchen tool, usually a mallet or a spiked device, used to physically break down the muscle fibers and connective tissue of tough cuts of meat to make it more tender
a kitchen tool used to grind whole peppercorns into a finer powder
a thick, insulated pad or cloth used in the kitchen to protect hands from hot cookware or to grip and handle hot pots, pans, and dishes safely
a cylindrical, usually wooden, kitchen tool with handles, used to flatten dough or pastry
a tool with many small holes and a wire net used for separating solid materials from smaller ones or liquids
a type of wire-mesh skimmer used to remove food items from hot liquids or oil, typically has a long handle and a shallow, wide wire basket similar to a spider's web
a kitchen tool designed to open metal cans by cutting through the top with a rotating blade and a serrated wheel
a strong, thin cord, often used in cooking to tie up meat or vegetables before cooking, or for bundling herbs or other food items together for easy removal from soups or stews
‌a handheld object with small pieces of curved wire used for whipping cream or eggs
a kitchen tool designed to remove the zest, or the colored outer layer of citrus fruit, such as lemons, limes, and oranges, in thin strips or shreds
a tool used to open cans of food
a small mesh or perforated device used to strain tea leaves or other solids from liquid tea when pouring from a teapot or teacup
a protective textile glove used to handle hot cookware or bakeware in the oven
a flat board or platter used to serve and display a variety of cheeses, often accompanied by other foods such as crackers, fruits, nuts, or meats
a large, sturdy, and durable wooden or plastic board used for chopping, slicing, and preparing food

examples.x: I placed the vegetables on the chopping block to start preparing the salad.
The wooden chopping block is perfect for cutting meat without damaging the knife.
The chef prefers a large chopping block for chopping multiple ingredients at once.
She always uses a separate chopping block for cutting raw meat to avoid cross-contamination.
After using the chopping block, I cleaned it thoroughly to remove any food scraps.

senses.definition: a kitchen tool used for slicing boiled eggs into even, thin slices
a handheld kitchen tool with two or more beaters or whisks used to beat, whisk, or mix ingredients by hand
a pair of scissors specifically designed for use in the kitchen, typically used for cutting food items such as herbs, meats, or packaging
a kitchen tool used for slicing fruits and vegetables with an adjustable blade
a kitchen utensil with standardized capacities used for measuring small amounts of ingredients
a kitchen tool designed to mash cooked vegetables, fruits, or other foods into a soft and uniform texture
a device used to measure the time that something takes, used when doing something such as cooking
a kitchen tool used for mashing and pureeing food into a fine and uniform texture, typically used for making mashed potatoes or baby food
a kitchen tool used in the production of cheese to press and shape the curds into a solid block or wheel
a kitchen tool used to store and dispense ground black pepper onto food

examples.x: I grabbed the pepper shaker and added some seasoning to my soup.
The pepper shaker was almost empty, so I refilled it with fresh pepper.
I prefer using a pepper shaker instead of grinding pepper directly onto my food.
The restaurant had a small pepper shaker at each table for customers to use.
He shook the pepper shaker over his salad to give it some extra flavor.

senses.definition: a container with a wide opening and a lid, typically made of glass or ceramic, used to store food such as honey, jam, pickles, etc.

examples.x: She carefully filled the jar with homemade strawberry jam, sealing it tightly with a lid.
The kitchen shelves were lined with colorful jars filled with spices, grains, and dried herbs.
He picked up the pickle jar from the pantry, intending to enjoy a tangy snack.
The candlelight flickered softly, casting shadows on the rows of jars that adorned the shelves.
With a gentle twist, she opened the honey jar, savoring its golden sweetness as it flowed onto her toast.

senses.definition: the part of a heater or cooker from which heat or a flame is produced

examples.x: She turned on the burner to heat the water for tea.
The burner on the stove wasn’t lighting properly.
She adjusted the burner to lower the flame while cooking.
He accidentally left the burner on after making breakfast.

senses.definition: a kitchen tool used to hold and dry dishes after washing
a shallow, rectangular or square-shaped container, often made of plastic, used for washing dishes by hand

examples.x: The dish pan is too large to fit in the sink, so we use a smaller one.
She filled the dish pan with warm soapy water to wash the dishes.
After dinner, he used the dish pan to scrub the pots and pans.
The dish pan was filled with soapy water, ready for cleaning the glasses.
He placed the dirty plates in the dish pan before washing them.

senses.definition: a container with a handle, lid, and spout that is used for boiling water

examples.x: She filled the kettle with water and put it on the stove.
He poured hot water from the kettle into his tea cup.
The kettle whistled loudly when the water was ready.
They bought a new stainless steel kettle for the kitchen.

senses.definition: a container with a handle, lid, and spout for brewing and serving tea

examples.x: She poured hot water into the teapot to brew her favorite herbal tea.
The ornate teapot was a cherished family heirloom, passed down through generations.
He carefully lifted the teapot by its handle to serve tea to their guests.
The teapot whistled, signaling that the water had reached the perfect temperature.
They bought a charming ceramic teapot during their trip to England.

senses.definition: a kitchen gadget used to turn vegetables or fruits into spiral shapes
a kitchen tool designed to quickly and easily slice fruits and vegetables into uniform pieces for cooking or serving

examples.x: The vegetable slicer made preparing the salad much faster.
She used the vegetable slicer to quickly cut the cucumbers into even slices.
He prefers using a vegetable slicer over a knife because it is safer and more efficient.
The vegetable slicer has adjustable settings to control the thickness of the slices.
I need to get a new vegetable slicer because mine broke last week.

senses.definition: a type of kitchenware used for making and serving coffee, usually made of metal or ceramic, and featuring a spout for pouring and a handle for carrying
an object that is used for cooking or eating

examples.x: Make sure you have the right utensils, like a spatula and whisk, for this recipe.
The drawer was filled with various utensils, from forks to can openers.
A sharp knife is one of the most essential utensils in the kitchen.
She couldn’t find the right utensil to flip the pancakes.
Wooden utensils are preferred for stirring sauces in non-stick pans.
